sourceMovie.title: Your Fault
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Your Fault (2024) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Fifty Shades Freed
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Your Fault clicked for you, Fifty Shades Freed (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Your Fault?
faq.answer: Try Fifty Shades Freed, Fifty Shades Darker, After We Collided—each shares DNA with Your Fault while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Your Fault?
faq.answer: Your Fault (2024) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Your Fault part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Fault Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
